title: Basic Text Structure
description: Assessment of the concept that text structure is how ideas are organized, and structure shapes meaning.

choices.text: The way ideas are organized in a text
choices.feedback: This is correct because text structure refers to how the author arranges ideas to build meaning.

choices.text: The length of each sentence in a paragraph
choices.feedback: Sentence length may affect style, but it is not the overall structure of the text.

choices.text: The topic a reader likes best
choices.feedback: Reader preference is not text structure.

choices.text: The number of difficult words in the article
choices.feedback: Vocabulary difficulty does not define how ideas are organized.

questions.prompt: A passage explains a problem with food waste at school and then describes a composting plan to solve it. Which text structure is used most clearly?

choices.feedback: The passage is not mainly comparing two subjects. It presents a problem and a solution.
There may be steps in the plan, but the main organization is a problem followed by a solution.
This is correct because the passage first identifies an issue and then explains how to address it.
The text does more than describe; it organizes ideas around a problem and a fix.

questions.prompt: Why does identifying text structure help a reader?

choices.text: It shows how the author builds meaning and connects ideas
choices.feedback: This is correct because structure helps readers understand how information is arranged and why it matters.

choices.text: It proves whether the author is always correct
choices.feedback: Text structure does not prove accuracy. It helps readers interpret organization.

choices.text: It tells readers which vocabulary words to ignore
choices.feedback: Text structure is about organization, not ignoring vocabulary.

choices.text: It replaces the need to identify a central idea
choices.feedback: Structure supports understanding, but readers still need to identify the central idea.

questions.prompt: A writer describes how the library changed before and after a renovation to show what improved. Which text structure best fits this approach?

choices.feedback: The passage may imply effects, but the main organization compares two versions of the library.
This is correct because the writer is showing differences between the library before and after renovation.
The organization is not mainly step-by-step in time; it is based on comparison.
The writer is not primarily defining a term.

questions.prompt: True or False: Text structure matters because the way ideas are arranged can shape how a reader understands the text.

choices.feedback: This is correct because organization influences how readers follow relationships among ideas.
This is incorrect because text structure does affect interpretation and understanding.
